title: Authentication, Sessions, Passwords, Bearer Tokens, and Credential Recovery · Runtime Contract: Cookie Session Security
summary: A service invents password hashing, trusts JWT payloads before verification, accepts any algorithm and audience, stores long-lived bearer tokens in logs, and leaves sessions valid after recovery. Planned artifact: Add and verify the httpsgo cookie session security increment in authentication and credential lifecycle policy. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Issue opaque sessions with rotation, server-side state or revocation, Secure, HttpOnly, SameSite, Path, Domain, expiry, fixation defense, and logout invalidation.
Use reviewed password hashing, bounded comparison, generic failures, rate and abuse controls, MFA or step-up policy, and secure recovery lifecycle.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
